heroDescription: Eulesia is social media built as citizen infrastructure — not a commercial platform. A public space where citizens and institutions meet under common rules.

whatIs.title: What is Eulesia?
whatIs.p1: Eulesia is social media with familiar features — feeds, messages, communities, and discussions — but without advertising, behavioral tracking, or attention economy. The name refers to the ancient agora: a public space where common affairs were discussed.
whatIs.p2: On Eulesia, municipalities, agencies, and other public institutions participate as civic actors, municipal meeting minutes are automatically brought in for discussion, and every conversation is linked to real decision-making processes.

whyNeeded.title: Why is this needed?
whyNeeded.p1: Public discourse has fragmented across commercial platforms designed to capture attention — not for constructive civic discussion. A municipality communicates on Facebook under Meta's rules, citizens comment on different platforms under different identities.
whyNeeded.p2: Existing participation platforms (Decidim, CONSUL) provide democratic structures but lack everyday social interaction. Commercial platforms offer social agency but without civic structure. Eulesia combines both.

fourSpaces.title: Four spaces
fourSpaces.agoraDesc: Public citizen discussion at three levels: local, national, and EU. AI summaries of municipal meeting minutes bring official content into discussion.
fourSpaces.clubsDesc: Free spaces for civil society and interest groups, where people can organize without institutional intermediaries.
fourSpaces.homeDesc: Private messaging and personal spaces. Content is not used for advertising or profiling.
fourSpaces.servicesDesc: Practical civic functions without attention economy logic. Coming later.

principles.title: Design principles
principles.identityDesc: One person, one account. Goal is integration with the European Digital Identity Wallet (EUDI).
principles.institutionalDesc: Public institutions participate as civic actors. Official documents, decisions, and contact information are visible in discussions.
principles.noAttentionDesc: No algorithmic feed, no infinite scroll, no engagement metrics. Your data is not a product.
principles.socialAgencyDesc: Citizens can organize and discuss freely without everything being channeled through institutional processes.
principles.privacyDesc: No advertising, no behavioral tracking, no data sales. GDPR built in.

automated.title: Automated civic content
automated.p1: Municipal meeting minutes are automatically fetched, summarized by the European Mistral language model, and published as discussion starters. There is always a direct link to original sources.
automated.p2: New users immediately encounter genuine civic content, and discussion begins while decisions are still being prepared — not only after they are made.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
nowItems: First early adopters via invite model
late2026Items: One person, one account principle enforced

funding.title: Funding
funding.description: Eulesia is a non-profit project. Funding sources:

organization.title: Organization
organization.p1: Eulesia is currently a private project developed by Markus Sjöberg. The long-term goal is a structure that ensures the platform's continuity and independence.
organization.p2: Possibilities being explored: an independent foundation, publicly funded organization, or university-based maintenance and development. The university model would enable research-based development and availability of anonymized social media data for European researchers.

team.title: Team
team.description: Development has so far been done by Markus Sjöberg. A real social media platform needs a team — if you're interested in joining, get in touch.

whatNot.title: What Eulesia is not
whatNot.notShareholder: Not accountable to shareholders (cf. Meta, X)
whatNot.notAdFunded: Not ad-funded — no business model based on behavioral data
whatNot.notUnilateral: No unilateral rule changes by private actors
whatNot.notEngagement: Not optimized for engagement, attention, or addiction

openSource.title: Open source
openSource.description: Eulesia's code is open source. Anyone can inspect, audit, and participate in development. Transparency is a core principle.

research.title: Research foundation
research.description: Eulesia's design is based on Design Science Research methodology. Design requirements are derived from platform governance research literature. The project is developed alongside academic research at Tampere University.

euAlignment.title: European digital public infrastructure
euAlignment.description: Designed in accordance with EU values: privacy, human dignity, democratic governance. Aligned with GDPR, DSA, and the EUDI Wallet framework.

cta.title: Interested?
cta.description: Eulesia is currently in an invite-only phase. If you know someone who is already a member, ask for an invite code.
